full_response:
We are given the Riemann tensor

R_{abcd}= -\frac{1}{\;l^{2}}\left(g_{ac}g_{bd}-g_{ad}g_{bc}\right) .      (1)

The Ricci tensor is obtained by contracting the first and third indices of the Riemann tensor:

R_{bd}=R^{a}{}_{bad}=g^{ac}R_{cabd}.                              (2)

Insert expression (1) into (2):

R_{bd}=g^{ac}\left[-\frac{1}{l^{2}}\left(g_{ca}g_{bd}-g_{cd}g_{ba}\right)\right]
     =-\frac{1}{l^{2}}\left[g^{ac}g_{ca}\,g_{bd}-g^{ac}g_{cd}\,g_{ba}\right].  (3)

Evaluate the two metric contractions:

1. g^{ac}g_{ca}=δ^{a}_{a}=n , where n is the dimension of spacetime (here n=4).

2. g^{ac}g_{cd}=δ^{a}_{d}.  Contracting this with g_{ba} gives δ^{a}_{d}g_{ba}=g_{bd}.

Substituting these results into (3):

R_{bd}= -\frac{1}{l^{2}}\left[ n\,g_{bd}-g_{bd}\right]
     = -\frac{1}{l^{2}}\bigl(n-1\bigr)g_{bd}.                       (4)

For a 4-dimensional spacetime (n=4):

R_{bd}= -\frac{3}{l^{2}}\,g_{bd}.                                   (5)

Thus the Ricci tensor is proportional to the metric with constant coefficient −3/l².

\boxed{R_{ab}= -\dfrac{3}{l^{2}}\,g_{ab}}

Confidence: 92%
